title: Tourist thanks 'legendary' people of Glasgow after almost missing flight

sections.heading: A stressful trip
sections.content: A tourist thanked people in Glasgow after a difficult journey to the airport nearly caused them and their mother to miss their flight home. Writing on Reddit's Glasgow forum, the traveller said they realised in a taxi from the Maldron Hotel that they were running late and had not yet checked in a suitcase.

sections.heading: Help at the airport
sections.content: The poster said the taxi driver got out when they reached the airport, left the cab with the doors open, carried some of their luggage and ran with them to the check-in area. They said passengers in the security queue then let them move ahead, while airport staff reassured them they would be fine and said the suitcase still needed to reach the plane.

sections.heading: Thanking Glasgow
sections.content: After making the flight, the tourist said they and their mother were struck by how welcoming people had been throughout the trip. They thanked the taxi driver, the passengers and airport staff, said their mother spoke no English and had still been helped patiently, and added that seeing the Glasgow fire from their hotel had broken their hearts.

sourceReferences.citationText: scotsman-news – Tourist thanks 'legendary' people of Glasgow after almost missing flight
